title: Habits not one-time things — self-eval (2022)
html:
Habits not one-time things — self-eval (2022)

Source: 2022-06-04 Apple Note. Habit-audit on himself — bad-habits column with named-friend triggers, good-habits column with self-claims.

TL;DR

  • Thesis: "One time things don't really matter — what matters in the end is the habits one has/constructs." Same lineage as the 2019 10 16 performance equation but evolved toward systems-thinking.
  • Bad habits called out: lying about himself, biting nails, relying on others for action — and the explicit triggers: "Gym Owen, playing stuff with yermy." Cross-references Owen and Yermy.
  • Maybe-bad ambivalence: "feel like I need to always improve" — first-time naming the fear of not doing as itself a possible pathology.
  • Good habits: "Can say no more than most. When I'm determined it's pretty hard to stop me. Keep my family in mind (drive). Aware our time is limited."
  • Hidden through-line: family + mortality as primary drivers, even at age 19 — the mom house by the river motivation already operational.

Connections

  • fear of not doing
  • owen davis
  • mom house by the river
  • osu
  • systems thinking

related.label: Life is meaningless without a pursuit — performance equation seed (2019)
